title: Stampede mechanics
description: A hot key's expiry is a synchronized burst, not a per-request event, multiplying origin load by concurrency.
body: Use when understanding why a hot key's expiry causes a backend spike. When a popular entry expires, every concurrent request misses simultaneously and independently recomputes the value, multiplying origin load by the request concurrency. Do not treat expiry as a per-request event — under load it is a coordinated burst. Actions: serialize recompute with a single-flight lock and serve stale during refill. Validation signals include origin calls per expiry approaching one. Risk boundary: an unbounded recompute lock can stall every reader if the origin is slow.
